The DTG gate forces the agent to continue exploring the remaining 2 frontiers until it is within 1.5 m of the goal before stop is allowed.
candidate_42.predicted_change: In XB4GS9ShBRE telemetry: '[T5_HOOKS_STOP] suppressed: dtg=X.Xm exceeds gate=1.5m' log lines appear immediately after the floor switch; stop_action calls at large DTG drop to zero; the episode continues with frontier-based exploration on the destination floor.
candidate_42.falsifiability_check: Log pattern '[T5_HOOKS_STOP] suppressed: dtg=<value> exceeds gate threshold' must appear for XB4GS9ShBRE post-floor-switch; no stop_action should fire while dtg > 1.5 m; episode must reach a second exploration phase rather than halting at the stair exit.
candidate_42.why_others_failed: Candidates 12–15, 20, 22, and 33 all targeted should_stop but received no scores, indicating parse/eval failures rather than a wrong concept. No candidate ever delivered a working DTG-gated should_stop implementation. This is the first concretely specified, single-condition DTG gate with an explicit numeric threshold.
candidate_42.why_this_will_work: The failure analysis for XB4GS9ShBRE explicitly lists 'dtg_gated_stop_guard' as the primary next_lever.
